question.text: Health insurance with lifelong renewability is recommended as a retirement planning strategy because:

answer.text: It provides higher returns than other investments
It protects the retirement corpus from being depleted by large medical expenses
It is mandatory under income tax laws
It eliminates all healthcare costs in retirement

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Lifelong renewable health insurance prevents large medical bills from eroding the retirement corpus. Healthcare costs escalate significantly with age and can be the single largest threat to retirement financial security. **The Trap:** Health insurance doesn't eliminate all costs (Option D) — deductibles, co-payments, and uncovered expenses still apply. It is not mandatory (Option C) but highly advisable. **Cross-Exam Context:** Health insurance as corpus protection is covered in Series XVII and connects to insurance planning in Series X-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Sign up for comprehensive health insurance with lifelong renewability in the late 40s or early 50s while still healthy. Premiums are lower and pre-existing condition exclusions are avoided.
